title: BAI President Addresses India Open Incident Involving Animals
indian_angle: The head of India's badminton association comments on a unique incident at a major Indian tournament.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma made a lighthearted comment regarding monkeys attending the India Open, stating "What is the problem if monkeys come to watch a game".

title: Indian Shuttlers Must Learn to Win Without Constant Coaching Presence
indian_angle: This opinion piece discusses the critical need for Indian badminton players to develop self-reliance and tactical thinking on court.
summary: An article suggests that Indian shuttlers need to develop the ability to think on their feet and win matches independently, even without a coach present, to succeed at higher levels.

title: Badminton India Implements New 3x15 Scoring System Domestically
indian_angle: This significant rule change will impact all junior domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in India.
summary: Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, debuting at Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: India's former World No. 1 Kidambi Srikanth showed strong form by reaching the final of the US Open.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open but was unable to secure the title, faltering at the last step against Su Li Yang.

title: Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan delivered a major upset by defeating the higher-ranked Chou Tien Chen.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a stir at the US Open by defeating World No 6 Chou Tien Chen.

title: BAI Warns Against Unauthorized Badminton Leagues and Events
indian_angle: The national governing body is advising players and officials to be cautious of unapproved tournaments, protecting the integrity of Indian badminton.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued an advisory warning against participation in unauthorized badminton leagues, tournaments, and championships, emphasizing the importance of official sanctioning.
